title: Ensure all non-privileged role assignments are periodically  reviewed

input_row.title: Ensure all non-privileged role assignments are periodically  reviewed
input_row.description: Perform a periodic review of non-privileged role assignments to ensure that the non- privileged roles assigned to users are appropriate. Note: Determining 'appropriate' assignments requires a clear understanding of your organization's personnel, systems, policies, and security requirements. This cannot be effectively prescribed in a procedure.
input_row.rationale: To ensure the principle of least privilege is followed, non-privileged role assignments should be reviewed periodically to confirm that users are granted only the minimum level of permissions they need to perform their tasks.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Subscriptions. 2. Click the name of a subscription. 3. Click Access control (IAM). 4. Click Role assignments. 5. Click Job function roles. 6. For each role, ensure the assignments are appropriate. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each subscription.
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Subscriptions. 2. Click the name of a subscription. 3. Click Access control (IAM). 4. Click Role assignments. 5. Click Job function roles. 6. Check the box next to any inappropriate assignments. 7. Click Delete. 8. Click Yes. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each subscription.
input_row.impact: Increased administrative effort to manage and remove role assignments appropriately.
input_row.default_value: Users do not have non-privileged roles assigned to them by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Open the AWS Management Console and navigate to the IAM service.
2. In the left-hand menu, click on "Roles" to view all existing IAM roles.
3. For each role that does not have administrative privileges, click on the role name to view its details.
4. Review the policy documents associated with the role to confirm whether the permissions granted align with the principle of least privilege.
5. Document any roles that have privileges that exceed the minimum necessary for the assigned users.
6. Repeat steps 3-5 for all non-privileged roles across your AWS accounts and regions that are relevant.
7. For each identified inappropriate role assignment, make a note of the roles and consider what permissions should be removed, if applicable. 
8. Ensure to periodically schedule these reviews (e.g., quarterly) to maintain compliance with the principle of least privilege.

By following these steps, you will be able to systematically review non-privileged role assignments and ensure they are appropriate according to the organization's policies and security requirements.
